mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, directly lifting S&P 500 valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in declining rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 6.5 percent, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress corporate earnings expectations, driving negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral when UNRATE crosses above 6.5%, or the signal's predictive power vanishes in post-2010 data.

mechanism_narrative: The joint movement of rising industrial production and falling housing starts indicates a supply-side bottleneck that squeezes corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when INDPRO rises and HOUST falls, or the interaction term shows zero correlation with forward returns in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-rate environment, rapid growth in real GDP accelerates inflationary pressures, forcing the Fed to maintain restrictive policy, which compresses equity multiples and yields negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive despite high GDP growth and restrictive rates, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% in backtesting.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust aggregate demand, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns show no positive correlation with PAYEMS growth, or the signal produces negative returns in more than 60% of positive PAYEMS months.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth decelerates below 2.0 percent annualized, it signals an approaching economic contraction, prompting risk-off positioning and negative equity returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth rate falls below 2.0 percent
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or rise when GDPC1 growth drops below 2.0%, or the threshold signal shows no statistical edge over a random baseline.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous rise in unemployment and increase in the federal funds rate creates a double squeeze on consumer spending and corporate borrowing costs, triggering a sharp decline in equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when both series rise, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of tight labor markets, rising industrial production fails to translate into higher corporate profits due to wage-push inflation, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive when INDPRO rises alongside PAYEMS in low-unemployment environments, or the signal's predictive power disappears when controlling for earnings revisions.
